content:
During the build syn artifact gets tracked by git temporarily (it disappears later) :

$ git status
HEAD detached at panfrost/tyr-for-upstream
Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
        rust/syn.d
I guess we should use rust/.gitignore not the root .gitignore for such files?

similar stuff on the C side:

$ git status
On branch tyr-reset-impl
Your branch and 'origin/tyr-reset-impl' have diverged,
and have 1 and 3 different commits each, respectively.
  (use "git pull" if you want to integrate the remote branch with yours)

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
        drivers/gpu/drm/nouveau/nouveau.ko.tmp17df952
        drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx4/mlx4_core.ko.tmp14ed431
        drivers/net/ethernet/mellanox/mlx5/core/mlx5_core.ko.tmp8b88fec
        drivers/net/ethernet/mscc/mscc_ocelot_switch_lib.ko.tmpa0845ff

This looks like an issue of our Makefile where the depinfo is moved into place rather than using --emit=depinfo=$(depinfo).

@Onur Özkan can you see if this fix the issue for you?

diff --git a/rust/Makefile b/rust/Makefile
index 629b3bdd2b20..e603b194366b 100644
--- a/rust/Makefile
+++ b/rust/Makefile
@@ -526,11 +526,10 @@ quiet_cmd_rustc_procmacrolibrary = $(RUSTC_OR_CLIPPY_QUIET) PL $@
       cmd_rustc_procmacrolibrary = \
    $(if $(skip_clippy),$(RUSTC),$(RUSTC_OR_CLIPPY)) \
        $(filter-out $(skip_flags),$(rust_common_flags) $(rustc_target_flags)) \
-       --emit=dep-info,link --crate-type rlib -O \
+       --emit=dep-info=$(depfile) --emit=link=$@ \
+       --crate-type rlib -O \
        --out-dir $(objtree)/$(obj) -L$(objtree)/$(obj) \
-       --crate-name $(patsubst lib%.rlib,%,$(notdir $@)) $<; \
-   mv $(objtree)/$(obj)/$(patsubst lib%.rlib,%,$(notdir $@)).d $(depfile); \
-   sed -i '/^\#/d' $(depfile)
+       --crate-name $(patsubst lib%.rlib,%,$(notdir $@)) $<

 $(obj)/libproc_macro2.rlib: private skip_clippy = 1
 $(obj)/libproc_macro2.rlib: private rustc_target_flags = $(proc_macro2-flags)

Our initial rust Makefile used the same approach, but it looks like in 295d8398c67e314d99bb070f38883f83fe94a97a Masahiro changes existing things to use explicit path emit. The comment removal is also no longer necessary after 2185242faddd12a1ba1060be5caf584fe5aba93a.
@Miguel Ojeda I assume the new addition of the removed pattern is an artifact of the syn introduction being a rebase of old work, so it doesn't pick up new paradigms introduced by Masahiro

@Onur Özkan can you see if this fix the issue for you?

will test it shortly
The patch seem to work. I tested it on a fresh-build with watch -n 1 git status and syn.ddidn't appear.

Gary Guo said:

Miguel Ojeda I assume the new addition of the removed pattern is an artifact of the syn introduction being a rebase of old work, so it doesn't pick up new paradigms introduced by Masahiro

I think you are right, yeah.
